manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: plan, lord, horse, life, road, club, school, number, president.
Your opponent's team words are: second, secret, phone, bit, building, side, woman, agent.
Distractor words are: tv, voice, light, week, court, mistake, buddy.
The kill words are: dream.

content.thinking:
Let me analyze the words I need to give clues for:

My team's words: plan, lord, horse, life, road, club, school, number, president
Opponent's words: second, secret, phone, bit, building, side, woman, agent
Distractors: tv, voice, light, week, court, mistake, buddy
Kill word: dream

I need to find a clue that connects multiple words from my team while avoiding connections to opponent words, distractors, and especially the kill word "dream".

Let me look for connections:

1. "plan, lord, horse, life, road, club, school, number, president" - these are all my targets

Possible groupings:
- "school, club, president" - could relate to education/organizations
- "horse, road" - transportation?
- "lord, president" - titles/leadership
- "life, number" - insurance? 
- "plan, road" - route/map

Let me think of good clues:

"MASTER" - could relate to: lord (master), plan (master plan), school (master's degree)
But need to check opponents: doesn't strongly relate to their words

"GAME" - could relate to: plan (game plan), horse (game piece like in chess), life (Game of Life), school (school game), club (game club)
Check opponents: doesn't strongly relate

"BUSINESS" - could relate to: plan (business plan), school (business school), club (business club), president (company president)
Check opponents: doesn't relate strongly

"HIGH" - could relate to: school (high school), road (high road), horse (high horse), life (high life), club (high club?), number (high number)
Check opponents: doesn't relate strongly

"FIVE" or "YEAR" - could relate to: plan (five-year plan), school (five years), president (five-year term in some countries)
But this seems weak

"VICE" - relates to president (vice president), but only one word


Let me explore compound words and phrases more systematically. "COUNTRY" could work with club (country club), road (country road), and life (country life), though it doesn't connect as strongly to president or lord. "OLD" might link school (old school), horse (old horse), and road (old road). I should also consider "YEAR" since it appears in phrases like five-year plan and could relate to multiple targets.

Looking at stronger candidates: "BATTLE" forms battle plan and battle horse (war horse), plus battle lord (warlord). "WAR" similarly creates war plan, war horse, and warlord. Both seem to connect across most of the target words without forcing connections.

"GAME" is particularly promising—it naturally pairs with game plan, Game of Life, game piece, game club, and school games, offering flexibility across multiple words. Reconsidering "COUNTRY" more carefully: it solidly works with lord (feudal lord), life (country life), road (country road), and club (country club), though some pairings like "country plan" or "country president" feel less natural. "HIGH" is worth exploring too, with high school and high road both being common phrases.

"PRIVATE" emerges as a strong candidate, working cleanly with private school, private club, private life, and private road—all standard compound terms without much interference from the opponent words. "BUSINESS" also shows promise with business plan and business school as clear matches, though some combinations like "business club" or "business life" are weaker.

Exploring other angles: "STRATEGIC" pairs well with plan but doesn't extend to the other words effectively. "COUNTRY" is particularly compelling—country club and country road are both very natural phrases, country life works well, and even lord connects through the feudal sense. The main weakness is that "country president" isn't idiomatic. Between these options, "COUNTRY" covering club, road, life, and lord seems like the strongest four-word connection.

Checking the opponent and distractor lists confirms none of them have strong associations with "country," and the kill word "dream" doesn't form a standard phrase either.

I'm settling on "COUNTRY" since it connects solidly to all four words: club, road, life, and lord. I considered alternatives like "VICE" (only works with president) and "BUSINESS" (strong with plan and school, but weak with the others), but "COUNTRY" gives me the most reliable four-word set.
